explicit_text: I was born in Mogadishu in December, and I grew up in a beautiful neighborhood where everyone knew each other. My childhood memories include visiting my great-grandfather who had many livestock like goats, cows, and sheep. Mogadishu was my home until the civil war began in 1991, which forced me and my family to leave for safety.
implicit_text: I was born in the capital city of Somalia in December, and I grew up in a beautiful neighborhood where everyone knew each other. My childhood memories include visiting my great-grandfather who had many livestock like goats, cows, and sheep. This city was my home until the civil war began in 1991, which forced me and my family to leave for safety.

explicit_text: The Somali Civil War started in 1991 when the government began to collapse and people started fighting each other. During those chaotic weeks, many relatives of mine, including my uncle, were killed and a lot of property was destroyed. This war forced me and my son to leave Somalia in 2002 to save our lives.

explicit_text: After fleeing Somalia, my son and I went to Cairo in Egypt in 2002 because we had a friend there. We lived peacefully in Cairo but struggled with missing essentials like food, clothes, and education. I found it very hard to support my son and to afford school because jobs were scarce for immigrants.

explicit_text: We lived in Egypt after leaving Somalia, specifically in Cairo, starting in 2002. entity: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
explicit_text: When I was pregnant and struggling in Egypt, I reached out to the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees for help. They have been assisting refugees worldwide for over five decades, and after waiting four years, they accepted me as a refugee. Thanks to them, I was able to move to the United States to start a new life.
implicit_text: When I was pregnant and struggling in that North African country, I reached out to the international agency responsible for protecting refugees worldwide for help. They have been assisting displaced people for over five decades, and after waiting four years, they accepted me as a refugee. Thanks to them, I was able to move to North America to start a new life.

explicit_text: After being accepted as a refugee, I moved to the United States, hoping my life would change immediately. However, it was still a struggle because I had no money and couldn't speak English. I worked hard, found a job, and now I balance studying and working while raising my children, always hoping for a better future here.
